root_text.an6.56:1.1: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā phagguno ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.an6.56:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.56:1.4: Sādhu, bhante, bhagavā yenāyasmā phagguno tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā”ti.
root_text.an6.56:1.6: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenāyasmā phagguno tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.an6.56:1.7: Addasā kho āyasmā phagguno bhagavantaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.an6.56:1.9: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ phaggunaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.56:1.11: Santimāni āsanāni parehi paññattāni, tatthāhaṁ nisīdissāmī”ti.
root_text.an6.56:1.13: Nisajja kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ phaggunaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.56:2.1: “Kacci te, phagguna, khamanīyaṁ kacci yāpanīyaṁ? Kacci te dukkhā vedanā paṭikkamanti, no abhikkamanti; paṭikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no abhikkamo”ti?
root_text.an6.56:2.2: “Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo.
root_text.an6.56:3.1: Seyyathāpi, bhante, balavā puriso tiṇhena sikharena muddhani abhimattheyya; evamevaṁ kho me, bhante, adhimattā vātā muddhani ūhananti.
root_text.an6.56:3.2: Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo.
root_text.an6.56:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhante, balavā puriso daḷhena varattakkhaṇḍena sīsaveṭhanaṁ dadeyya; evamevaṁ kho me, bhante, adhimattā sīse sīsavedanā.
root_text.an6.56:4.2: Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo.
root_text.an6.56:5.1: Seyyathāpi, bhante, dakkho goghātako vā goghātakantevāsī vā tiṇhena govikantanena kucchiṁ parikanteyya; evamevaṁ kho me, bhante, adhimattā vātā kucchiṁ parikantanti.
root_text.an6.56:5.2: Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo.
root_text.an6.56:6.1: Seyyathāpi, bhante, dve balavanto purisā dubbalataraṁ purisaṁ nānābāhāsu gahetvā aṅgārakāsuyā santāpeyyuṁ samparitāpeyyuṁ; evamevaṁ kho me, bhante, adhimatto kāyasmiṁ ḍāho.
root_text.an6.56:6.2: Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo”ti.
root_text.an6.56:6.3: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ phaggunaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassetvā samādapetvā samuttejetvā sampahaṁsetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
root_text.an6.56:7.1: Atha kho āyasmā phagguno acirapakkantassa bhagavato kālamakāsi.
root_text.an6.56:7.2: Tamhi cassa samaye maraṇakāle indriyāni vippasīdiṁsu.
root_text.an6.56:7.3: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.56:7.5: Tamhi cassa samaye maraṇakāle indriyāni vippasīdiṁsū”ti.
root_text.an6.56:8.1: “Kiṁ hānanda, phaggunassa bhikkhuno indriyāni na vippasīdissanti.
root_text.an6.56:8.2: Phaggunassa, ānanda, bhikkhuno pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ avimuttaṁ ahosi.
root_text.an6.56:8.3: Tassa taṁ dhammadesanaṁ sutvā pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ vimuttaṁ.
root_text.an6.56:9.1: Chayime, ānanda, ānisaṁsā kālena dhammassavane kālena atthupaparikkhāya.
root_text.an6.56:9.3: Idhānanda, bhikkhuno pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ avimuttaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.56:9.4: So tamhi samaye maraṇakāle labhati tathāgataṁ dassanāya.
root_text.an6.56:9.5: Tassa tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāseti.
root_text.an6.56:9.6: Tassa taṁ dhammadesanaṁ sutvā pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.an6.56:9.7: Ayaṁ, ānanda, paṭhamo ānisaṁso kālena dhammassavane.
root_text.an6.56:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, ānanda, bhikkhuno pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ avimuttaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.56:10.2: So tamhi samaye maraṇakāle na heva kho labhati tathāgataṁ dassanāya, api ca kho tathāgatasāvakaṁ labhati dassanāya.
root_text.an6.56:10.3: Tassa tathāgatasāvako dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāseti.
root_text.an6.56:10.4: Tassa taṁ dhammadesanaṁ sutvā pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.an6.56:10.5: Ayaṁ, ānanda, dutiyo ānisaṁso kālena dhammassavane.
root_text.an6.56:11.1: Puna caparaṁ, ānanda, bhikkhuno pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ avimuttaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.56:11.2: So tamhi samaye maraṇakāle na heva kho labhati tathāgataṁ dassanāya, napi tathāgatasāvakaṁ labhati dassanāya;
root_text.an6.56:11.3: api ca kho yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ cetasā anuvitakketi anuvicāreti manasānupekkhati.
root_text.an6.56:11.4: Tassa yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ cetasā anuvitakkayato anuvicārayato manasānupekkhato pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.an6.56:11.5: Ayaṁ, ānanda, tatiyo ānisaṁso kālena atthupaparikkhāya.
root_text.an6.56:12.1: Idhānanda, bhikkhuno pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti, anuttare ca kho upadhisaṅkhaye cittaṁ avimuttaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.56:12.2: So tamhi samaye maraṇakāle labhati tathāgataṁ dassanāya.
root_text.an6.56:12.3: Tassa tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ …pe… brahmacariyaṁ pakāseti.
root_text.an6.56:12.4: Tassa taṁ dhammadesanaṁ sutvā anuttare upadhisaṅkhaye cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.an6.56:12.5: Ayaṁ, ānanda, catuttho ānisaṁso kālena dhammassavane.
root_text.an6.56:13.1: Puna caparaṁ, ānanda, bhikkhuno pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti, anuttare ca kho upadhisaṅkhaye cittaṁ avimuttaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.56:13.2: So tamhi samaye maraṇakāle na heva kho labhati tathāgataṁ dassanāya, api ca kho tathāgatasāvakaṁ labhati dassanāya.
root_text.an6.56:13.3: Tassa tathāgatasāvako dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ …pe… parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāseti.
root_text.an6.56:13.4: Tassa taṁ dhammadesanaṁ sutvā anuttare upadhisaṅkhaye cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.an6.56:13.5: Ayaṁ, ānanda, pañcamo ānisaṁso kālena dhammassavane.
root_text.an6.56:14.1: Puna caparaṁ, ānanda, bhikkhuno pañcahi orambhāgiyehi saṁyojanehi cittaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti, anuttare ca kho upadhisaṅkhaye cittaṁ avimuttaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.56:14.2: So tamhi samaye maraṇakāle na heva kho labhati tathāgataṁ dassanāya, napi tathāgatasāvakaṁ labhati dassanāya;
root_text.an6.56:14.3: api ca kho yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ cetasā anuvitakketi anuvicāreti manasānupekkhati.
root_text.an6.56:14.4: Tassa yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ cetasā anuvitakkayato anuvicārayato manasānupekkhato anuttare upadhisaṅkhaye cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.an6.56:14.5: Ayaṁ, ānanda, chaṭṭho ānisaṁso kālena atthupaparikkhāya.
root_text.an6.56:15.1: Ime kho, ānanda, cha ānisaṁsā kālena dhammassavane kālena atthupaparikkhāyā”ti.

translation_text.an6.56:1.1: Now at that time Venerable Phagguna was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.an6.56:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an6.56:1.4: Sir, please go to Venerable Phagguna out of sympathy.”
translation_text.an6.56:1.6: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to Venerable Phagguna.
translation_text.an6.56:1.7: Venerable Phagguna saw the Buddha coming off in the distance
translation_text.an6.56:1.10: “It’s all right, Phagguna, don’t get up.
translation_text.an6.56:1.11: There are some seats spread out by others, I will sit there.”
translation_text.an6.56:1.12: He sat on the seat spread out
translation_text.an6.56:2.1: “I hope you’re keeping well, Phagguna; I hope you’re all right. And I hope the pain is fading, not growing, that its fading is evident, not its growing.”
translation_text.an6.56:2.2: “Sir, I’m not keeping well, I’m not getting by. The pain is terrible and growing, not fading; its growing is evident, not its fading.
translation_text.an6.56:3.1: The winds piercing my head are so severe, it feels like a strong man drilling into my head with a sharp point.
translation_text.an6.56:4.1: The pain in my head is so severe, it feels like a strong man tightening a strong leather strap around my head.
translation_text.an6.56:5.1: The winds slicing my belly are so severe, like a deft butcher or their apprentice were slicing open a cows’s belly with a sharp meat cleaver.
translation_text.an6.56:6.1: The burning in my body is so severe, it feels like two strong men grabbing a weaker man by the arms to burn and scorch him on a pit of glowing coals.
translation_text.an6.56:6.2: I’m not keeping well, I’m not getting by. The pain is terrible and growing, not fading; its growing is evident, not its fading.”
translation_text.an6.56:6.3: Then the Buddha educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired Venerable Phagguna with a Dhamma talk, after which he got up from his seat and left.
translation_text.an6.56:7.1: Not long after the Buddha left, Venerable Phagguna passed away.
translation_text.an6.56:7.2: At the time of his death, his faculties were bright and clear.
translation_text.an6.56:7.3: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an6.56:7.4: “Sir, soon after the Buddha left, Venerable Phagguna died.
translation_text.an6.56:7.5: At the time of his death, his faculties were bright and clear.”
translation_text.an6.56:8.1: “And why shouldn’t his faculties be bright and clear?
translation_text.an6.56:8.2: The mendicant Phagguna’s mind was not freed from the five lower fetters.
translation_text.an6.56:8.3: But when he heard that teaching his mind was freed from them.
translation_text.an6.56:9.1: Ānanda, there are these six benefits to hearing the teaching at the right time and examining the meaning at the right time.
translation_text.an6.56:9.3: Firstly, take the case of a mendicant whose mind is not freed from the five lower fetters.
translation_text.an6.56:9.4: At the time of death they get to see the Realized One.
translation_text.an6.56:9.5: The Realized One proclaims a teaching to them that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And he reveals a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an6.56:9.6: When they hear that teaching their mind is freed from the five lower fetters.
translation_text.an6.56:9.7: This is the first benefit of listening to the teaching.
translation_text.an6.56:10.1: Next, take the case of another mendicant whose mind is not freed from the five lower fetters.
translation_text.an6.56:10.2: At the time of death they don’t get to see the Realized One, but they get to see a Realized One’s disciple.
translation_text.an6.56:10.3: The Realized One’s disciple teaches them Dhamma …
translation_text.an6.56:10.4: When they hear that teaching their mind is freed from the five lower fetters.
translation_text.an6.56:10.5: This is the second benefit of listening to the teaching.
translation_text.an6.56:11.1: Next, take the case of another mendicant whose mind is not freed from the five lower fetters.
translation_text.an6.56:11.2: At the time of death they don’t get to see the Realized One, or to see a Realized One’s disciple.
translation_text.an6.56:11.3: But they think about and consider the teaching in their heart, examining it with the mind as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an6.56:11.4: As they do so their mind is freed from the five lower fetters.
translation_text.an6.56:11.5: This is the third benefit of listening to the teaching.
translation_text.an6.56:12.1: Next, take the case of a mendicant whose mind is freed from the five lower fetters, but not with the supreme ending of attachments.
translation_text.an6.56:12.2: At the time of death they get to see the Realized One.
translation_text.an6.56:12.3: The Realized One teaches them Dhamma …
translation_text.an6.56:12.4: When they hear that teaching their mind is freed with the supreme ending of attachments.
translation_text.an6.56:12.5: This is the fourth benefit of listening to the teaching.
translation_text.an6.56:13.1: Next, take the case of another mendicant whose mind is freed from the five lower fetters, but not with the supreme ending of attachments.
translation_text.an6.56:13.2: At the time of death they don’t get to see the Realized One, but they get to see a Realized One’s disciple.
translation_text.an6.56:13.3: The Realized One’s disciple teaches them Dhamma …
translation_text.an6.56:13.4: When they hear that teaching their mind is freed with the supreme ending of attachments.
translation_text.an6.56:13.5: This is the fifth benefit of listening to the teaching.
translation_text.an6.56:14.1: Next, take the case of another mendicant whose mind is freed from the five lower fetters, but not with the supreme ending of attachments.
translation_text.an6.56:14.2: At the time of death they don’t get to see the Realized One, or to see a Realized One’s disciple.
translation_text.an6.56:14.3: But they think about and consider the teaching in their heart, examining it with the mind as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an6.56:14.4: As they do so their mind is freed with the supreme ending of attachments.
translation_text.an6.56:14.5: This is the sixth benefit of listening to the teaching.
translation_text.an6.56:15.1: These are the six benefits to hearing the teaching at the right time and examining the meaning at the right time.”

variant_text.an6.56:3.1: muddhani → muddhānaṁ (bj, pts1ed) | abhimattheyya → abhimatteyya (si); abhimaṭṭheyya (sya-all) | muddhani → muddhānaṁ (bj, pts1ed) | ūhananti → hananti (bj, pts1ed); ohananti (sya-all)
variant_text.an6.56:8.1: hānanda, phaggunassa → hānanda phagguṇassa (sya-all); pheggunassa ānanda (mr)
variant_text.an6.56:10.2: na heva kho → no ca kho (mr)
